evidence.description: The diversity of approaches. You can see more at: https://fr.pinterest.com/alternatiview/installation-art-sculpture-contemporary-art/ or at: https://www.pinterest.fr/alternatiview/

Your new post is loading...
Your new post is loading...
Scooped by
Anne Bak
January 29, 2013 5:50 AM
Konstantin Dimopoulos: Blue trees, Burke-Gilman Regional Trail, Kenmore, Washington State, USA
"The Blue Trees is a social art action. Through colour I am making a personal statement about the spirituality of trees and their importance to our very survival: trees are the lungs of the planet.
Colour is a powerful stimulant, a means of altering perception and defining space and time. The fact that blue is a colour that is not naturally identified with trees suggests to the viewer that something unusual, something out of the ordinary has happened. It becomes a magical transformation.
In nature colour is used both as a defensive mechanism, a means of protection, and as a mechanism to attract. The Blue Trees attempts to waken a similar response from viewers. It is within this context that the blue denotes sacredness, something reverential.
Trees are largely invisible in our daily lives, and it’s not until it’s too late that we realise how important they are to us both aesthetically and environmentally. Each year an area at least the size of Belgium of native forests is cleared from around the planet.
Yet while we do this we look at whether other planets can be inhabited, so we’ve got somewhere else to go once we’ve destroyed our own.
The colour used on the trees is biologically safe pigmented water. As an ephemeral artwork, the colour will naturally degrade and the trees gradually revert to their natural state." K.D.
http://www.kondimopoulos.com /

Scooped by
Anne Bak
September 27, 2012 5:51 PM
Kimihiko Okada has created an Aluminum Landscape that incorporates changes in nature into the installation. This public space project, located outside at the Museum of Contemporary Art in Tokyo, is a hollow structure of a thin membrane of aluminum supported by a system of metal posts. The result is an installation resembling a shiny mountain range, on a smaller scale. The sun and the sky are reflected; rain and wind have a physical impact.
www.ookd.jp /
Scooped by
Anne Bak
April 28, 2012 11:10 PM
VIDEO
Wela: "Whisperers", interactive audio installation, 2011, Copernicus Science Center, Discovery Park, Warsaw, Poland
Installation "The Whisperers" consists of 16 stainless steel columns put in the middle of the park of discovery. The columns are equipped with sensors and speakers in order to detect visitors and distribute natural sounds such as the whisper of a stream, rain, wind, volcano, etc. People can do real-time sound spatialization on the type of sound and played on the sound effects. The visual and sound installation, compared to contemporary organs, symbolizes a virtual-real time in which we live and transports the viewer to the fourth more spiritual dimension. The installation also shows the paradox of contemporary man desire to approach to nature by artificial means.
http://www.wela-art.com /
